formulas.name: Non-Aligned Movement (NAM) Principles
formulas.formula: Non-alignment, Non-aggression, Non-intervention, Non-expansionism, Non-interference, and Non-dependence.
formulas.notes: These principles guide the NAM in its relations with other countries.

formulas.name: Panchsheel Principles
formulas.formula: Mutual respect for each other's territorial integrity and sovereignty, mutual non-aggression, mutual non-interference in domestic affairs, mutual non-interference in internal affairs, peaceful coexistence, and mutual benefits.
formulas.notes: These principles are a set of five principles agreed upon by India and China in 1954 to guide their relations.

formulas.name: Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) Principles
formulas.formula: Non-aggression, non-interference, and non-expansionism, respect for sovereignty and territorial integrity of all countries, and mutual trust, beneficial cooperation, and peaceful coexistence.
formulas.notes: These principles guide the SCO in its relations with member countries.

formulas.name: ASEAN Way
formulas.notes: These principles guide ASEAN in its relations with member countries.

formulas.name: Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence
formulas.formula: Mutual respect for each other's territorial integrity and sovereignty, mutual non-aggression, mutual non-interference in domestic affairs, mutual non-interference in internal affairs, and peaceful coexistence.
formulas.variables: Core principles of Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence.
formulas.notes: These principles were proposed by India and China in 1954 and were also adopted by the UN General Assembly in 1970.
